principles: Realize the cornerstone: link traversal ADDS structurally-related context vector search missed (additive, not displacing top hits).
Bounded by query relevance + global top-M, NOT by out-degree (hubs link to 500+ docs).
Driven by profile.retrieval.graph; max_hops=0 reproduces today's behaviour exactly (ablation toggle).
Custom Cypher extension (per LIR-008 spike), one bounded expansion query; verify live on the real graph.
Expanded nodes are attributable (via_link / linked_from) and decayed so direct hits rank first.

tasks.title: Bounded query-relevant 1-hop LINKS_TO expansion in HierarchicalPGRetriever
acceptance_criteria: HierarchicalPGRetriever reads graph config (max_hops, edge_types, expand_m, link_decay); build_hierarchical_retriever passes profile.retrieval.graph
When max_hops>0: one expansion Cypher follows LINKS_TO from seed docs, scores neighbour leaf chunks via vector.similarity.cosine, takes per-neighbour top-1 then GLOBAL top-M
Expanded NodeWithScore: deduped vs direct hits, score = link_decay*sim, metadata via_link=True + linked_from=<seed source_url> + source_url/doc_id
Additive output (k direct + up to M expanded); max_hops=0 == current behaviour exactly
GraphRetrievalConfig gains expand_m (default 5) + link_decay (default 0.5); neo4j_hier.yaml documents them; ruff clean

tasks.title: Tests: unit (fake store) + live verify on the real graph
acceptance_criteria: Unit test (fake store returning seed + neighbour chunks): asserts expansion fires, decay applied, dedup vs direct, global M-cap, and max_hops=0 disables it
Live verify on the built graph: a query whose seed doc has LINKS_TO neighbours returns >=1 via_link node with provenance, scored below direct hits; record latency vs the no-expansion path
Full indexing test suite + ruff green

tasks.title: Query-cache hygiene (clear stale pre-refactor entries + disable toggle)
acceptance_criteria: The 15 stale pre-refactor query-cache entries are cleared (they answer over the deleted corpus)
A way to bypass the cache short-circuit for clean retrieval testing (e.g. EMA_QUERY_CACHE_DISABLED=1 honoured in app.py)
Documented so a fresh chat exercises the new retriever instead of serving cached old answers

notes: Independent of work unit 20 (closed). LTG-001 is the substantive cornerstone fix; LTG-003 is independent and quick. One non-blocking design choice (neighbour-chunk = query-relevant top-1, M=5, decay=0.5) — defaults chosen, confirm/adjust at implementation.
superseded_by: 2026-06-03_23_native-llamaindex-retrieval (custom-Cypher links expansion dropped per native-only directive; the gap it found is now solved natively there)
